The Rubus genus, including species like Rubus fruticosus, consists of a group of flowering plants in the rose family known as brambles or raspberries. The health benefits of Rubus species include their high content of antioxidants, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation, as well as their potential to support heart health and lower blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Rubus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Rubus species are commonly used to make teas, jams, and supplements, as well as being used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including as a treatment for digestive issues and to support wound healing.
